name: Pico Vizmaya Trail
name_original: Pico Vizmaya – circular por la Iglesia de Santa Marina
description: Hike the easy 3.4-mile Pico Vizmaya Trail in Cantabria, Spain, gaining 627 feet for panoramic views of Santander Bay.
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Lic-Rio Miera offer access to a diverse landscape characterized by rugged topography, extensive karst formations, and the Miera River valley. The region features significant beech and holm oak forests, alongside glacial landforms such as circuses and moraines. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences, from gentle river walks to more challenging ascents. Best bus station hiking trails around Lic-Rio Miera The most popular bus station hiking route is Pico Vizmaya Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This easy route features an elevation gain of 191 meters. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This route involves a substantial elevation gain of 992 meters, offering views of the surrounding peaks. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from La Pedrosa, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Lic-Rio Miera is defined by its karst formations, lush beech forests, and riverine landscapes shaped by the Miera River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to demanding mountain trails. The routes in Lic-Rio Miera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Miera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are accessible from bus stations in the Lic-Rio Miera region?
A: There are over 65 hiking trails in the Lic-Rio Miera region that are easily accessible from bus stations, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see on bus-accessible hikes in Lic-Rio Miera?
A: The Lic-Rio Miera region is known for its diverse and striking natural landscapes. You can expect to encounter rugged topography with sharp, bare rocks, extensive karst formations like sinkholes, and lush forests, particularly well-preserved beech and holm oak groves. Many routes also follow the Miera River, offering scenic riverine views.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking routes accessible by bus in Lic-Rio Miera?
A: Yes, the region offers many easy routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31593768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castaño de Rioz – Heras Reservoir loop from Medio Cudeyo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy hike covering about 7.2 km with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes that start and end at a bus station?
A: Absolutely. Many trails are designed as loops, making them convenient for public transport users. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.8 km route that brings you back to your starting point.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see on these trails?
A: The trails often lead past significant natural and historical sites. You might encounter impressive geological formations, ancient forests, or viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguanaz River and Frenchman’s Cave&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3973630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguanaz River Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Gate&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes accessible by bus for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996940056/pico-porracolina-via-calseca-stream-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.3 km hike with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views for those up to the task.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Lic-Rio Miera, especially from bus stations?
A: The Miera Valley is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn showcases stunning fall foliage in the extensive beech and holm oak forests. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation trails provide cooler conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails accessible by bus?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Lic-Rio Miera region, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always check local signage for specific restrictions, and remember to clean up after your pet. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996912060/pico-vizmaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Vizmaya Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that can be enjoyed with a canine companion.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lic-Rio Miera?
A: The Lic-Rio Miera region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to lush forests, and the well-preserved natural beauty. Many appreciate the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels and the accessibility via public transport.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest along the bus-accessible routes?
A: Yes, the Miera Valley is rich in cultural heritage. Beyond natural beauty, some routes incorporate secular and religious architectural heritage. You might encounter traditional 'Pas cottages' or historical buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Miera-Rubalcaba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poor Clares of Villaverde de Pontones&lt;/a&gt;, offering insights into the traditional 'pasiego' way of life.

Although it is a very short route, it depends on the paths you take inside the mountain, you will need some hiking boots and even a stick, as some paths are very steep and muddy, it is important to take this into account if it rains or it has rained a couple of days before and no sun.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pico Vizmaya Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered easy to moderate. It involves a gentle ascent through varied vegetation, with some sections featuring loose stones. It's suitable for a shorter outing and accessible to most hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain includes paths through various types of vegetation and some sections with loose stones. You'll also encounter areas shaped by historical iron mining, which can create a unique 'lunar landscape' effect in places.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You can visit the historic Palacio de los Acevedo and the Church of Santa Marina. The summit of Pico Vizmaya provides panoramic views. The trail also passes by historical iron mines and a quarry.

faqs.question: What views can I expect from the summit?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Pico Vizmaya, you are rewarded with panoramic views of Santander Bay, many mountains of Cantabria, the Trasmiera valley, and the nearby Sierra Cabarga massif.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Senda al Pico Vizmaya, Camino de Guadarmino, and Camino del Barrio El Convento a Santa Marina.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Vizmaya Trail suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its easy to moderate difficulty and relatively short distance, this trail is generally suitable for families looking for a pleasant outdoor activity.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific rules are not detailed, dogs are generally permitted on trails in this region. It's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near historical sites or if livestock is present.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also a good option, though it can be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially cooler and wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Pico Vizmaya Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not provided, but access points are typically found near Santa Marina or Entrambasaguas. Look for designated parking areas in these villages.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail?
faqs.answer: No permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Pico Vizmaya Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near villages like Santa Marina and Entrambasaguas, where you can find local cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options.
